title: Tamayorkis ehrenbergii
intro: Tamayorkis ehrenbergii, also known as the Desert Marigold or Mexican Gold Poppy, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to ease congestion and promote healing. The bioactive constituents of Tamayorkis ehrenbergii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbalism, Tamayorkis ehrenbergii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
